Financial planning

Remember the last time you bought something at a discount, only to see it later for the same price without the discount? Or when the product costs more today than it did yesterday. Did you feel cheated? Are you ready to look behind the screen of manipulation and lies? Spycost helps you see when you’re paying more while thinking you’re paying less. It gives you the full price picture, so you can decide whether it’s actually worth it.

Free Options

Launch tags:Productivity•Finance•Shopping [...] Hey Product Hunt 👋 I built Spycost because I couldn’t build a clear purchase priority list while being constantly hit with “attractive offers.” I kept breaking my own priorities because I was buying less important things just because I saw a “discount”. And later, I kept realizing that the discount I fell for was fake, or not nearly as good as it was presented. I constantly felt stupid, like I had been robbed by thieves I opened the door for and invited into my home myself. Spycost is a price tracker that shows you when you’re being fooled, or when the right moment to buy has finally come. At the end of the day, the final choice is still yours. If you don’t need to buy something right here and now, but you’re only planning to buy it, this is the perfect option for you. I built Spycost for [...] Report

@adana thank you! I felt the same problem myself. Discounts often make it harder to tell whether I’m making a good decision or just reacting to the label. Spycost started as a small attempt to make that a bit easier to see.

Report

Tehreem Fatima

@glebarios Saw the link in the community on LinkedIn and just had to check it out! This idea really appealed to me because impulse buying due to 'fake discounts' is such a relatable problem. Love how you're making things so much easier and transparent for buyers. You definitely have my support, Hlib! Good luck with the launch!

# SpycostAlternatives

Fell for a discount again? If Spycost isn’t enough, try Honey for automatic coupons, Coupert for cashback plus price comparison, Chestr to track wishlists and drops, and Price Tracker for bottom-line alerts. Combine them to time purchases, stack codes, and avoid overpaying.

results.title: Turn off the Eiffel Tower
results.content:
🔥 Our Take: Every shopping tool you've ever installed is on the store's side. Honey exists to complete the purchase. This is the rare one built to talk you out of it: Hlib's sharpest line in the thread is that a discount can feel like information when it's really just pressure wrapped in a nice label. Spycost doesn't block or decide anything, it just shows you the price's history and lets you draw the obvious conclusion. The cheapest purchase is still the one you skip.

## That discount is lying to you

Spycost is a price tracker with one job: telling you whether that sale is real. Hlib Sukhovoi (@glebarios) built it after one fake discount too many left him feeling, in his words, robbed by thieves he'd invited into his own home. The mascot is a little duck that watches prices for you. [...] newsletter icon

July 19th, 2026

# Turn off the Eiffel Tower

## Turn off the Eiffel Tower

gm legends, happy Sunday.

Today: a world map with a filter that deletes famous places, open source comes for your $300 SEO bill, and a duck that knows that discount is fake.

## This map hides famous places

Alban (@flightmussy), whose whole Product Hunt bio is "creating stuff for fun," built Detourmap: one interactive map of 70,000 places pulled from Wikidata, from waterfalls and catacombs to 584 ghost towns, each ranked on a fame scale that runs from world-famous down to hidden gem. Free, no account, no ads.
